text: Good result. I got the three safest phrases fast, and the corrections were clear. A little too much extra Klingon at the start, but it stayed practical.
Pretty satisfying overall—I got the cultural distinctions I was after and had something to repeat back, though a few of the Klingon examples felt a bit shaky or inconsistent.
Pretty good. Fast, practical, and it kept confirming my lines, which is exactly what I needed.

Pretty good overall—I got the core phrases and cultural context I wanted, and I liked the encouragement to repeat them back. A few of the Klingon forms seemed a bit shaky or inconsistent, so I’d rate it as helpful but not fully reliable.
I got a lot of useful practice and the cultural explanation felt genuinely helpful; I’d just have liked a slightly cleaner, more confident treatment of the everyday “How are you?” phrasing.
Pretty satisfying — I got the greeting, the cultural framing, and a usable little intro dialogue, though a few of the grammar explanations felt a bit shaky and inconsistent.
